description: US-05 Record Attendance and Payment — One action records customer, date, showed-up and paid, with the price that applied, derived per head. One record per customer per day, enforced by the database; records survive indefinitely.

userStories.title: Attendance rules — duplicate prevention and same-day correction
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the attendance rules as pure functions, so duplicate prevention and same-day correction are testable without a database. Source: tasks/prd-us-05-record-attendance.md §US-05.1
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/distribution/attendance.ts exports canRecord(existingRecordsForCustomer, today) returning OK or a typed AlreadyServedToday error carrying the existing record's date
canCorrect(record, today) returns true only when record.date is the same calendar day as today
Day comparison is calendar-day based in Europe/Berlin and documented in a comment
Tests cover a midnight boundary and a DST change
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented src/domain/distribution/attendance.ts (canRecord/canCorrect) with a Europe/Berlin calendar-day key via Intl; added AlreadyServedToday domain error (replacing the unused DuplicateAttendance code). Tests cover midnight boundary and both DST transitions. All gates green.

userStories.title: recordAttendance and correctAttendance use cases
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want one action that records the hand-out, so the queue keeps moving. Source: tasks/prd-us-05-record-attendance.md §US-05.2
acceptanceCriteria: recordAttendance(deps, {customerId, paid}) writes one record with the customer's surrogate id, deps.clock.now(), showedUp true, the paid flag and priceCents resolved from the settings in force on that date
paid defaults to true and can be passed as false
A second invocation for the same customer on the same day rejects with AlreadyServedToday and writes nothing
The use case re-evaluates the counter verdict before writing and refuses ARCHIVED, BLOCKED and WRONG_GROUP — the UI is not the only guard
correctAttendance(deps, {recordId, ...}) amends or deletes a record made today; an older record rejects with a typed RecordNoLongerCorrectable error
Both write audit entries with no actor field
Tested against hand-written fakes and a fake clock, including the same-day duplicate and next-day correction cases
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added recordAttendance + correctAttendance use cases (src/application/distribution/). recordAttendance re-runs evaluateAtCounter (refuses ARCHIVED/BLOCKED/WRONG_GROUP via new NotClearToServe error carrying the verdict), runs canRecord (throws AlreadyServedToday, writes nothing), resolves priceCents via describeAllowance at now, writes one record + a no-actor audit entry (distribution.recorded); paid defaults true. correctAttendance takes a discriminated {action:'SET_PAID'|'REMOVE'} input, guarded by canCorrect (RecordNoLongerCorrectable for older records, DistributionRecordNotFound when absent). New domain type src/domain/distribution/distributionRecord.ts; new DistributionRecordRepository port. Tested against hand-written fakes + fake clock incl. same-day duplicate and next-day correction. All gates green (417 tests, 100% coverage).

userStories.title: DistributionRecord schema with the one-per-day constraint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need distribution records that survive indefinitely and cannot be duplicated per day. Source: tasks/prd-us-05-record-attendance.md §US-05.3
acceptanceCriteria: Prisma model DistributionRecord: id, customerId (FK to Customer.id), date, showedUp, paid, priceCents Int
A normalised date-only day-key column carries a unique constraint on (customerId, dayKey) so the database prevents a double record
Indexes on date and on (customerId, date)
No delete cascade from customer archiving — records outlive status changes
Integration test proves the duplicate constraint fires
A schema comment records that priceCents is deliberate redundancy alongside the settings history, so it is not 'cleaned up' later
Migration committed under prisma/migrations/
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added the Prisma DistributionRecord model (id, customerId FK, date, dayKey, showedUp, paid, priceCents) and PrismaDistributionRecordRepository. The unique (customerId, dayKey) index enforces once-per-day; dayKey is the Europe/Berlin calendar day written by the domain's now-exported berlinDayKey, so the DB constraint and attendance.canRecord share one rule. Indexes on date and (customerId, date) for the US-10 no-show query. Relation has no onDelete cascade (RESTRICT) — records outlive status changes; a schema comment marks priceCents as deliberate redundancy. Regenerated the disposable init migration and re-added the hand-written partial slot unique index. Integration test (15 cases) proves the duplicate constraint fires as AlreadyServedToday, the Berlin-midnight boundary is two days, the constraint is per-customer, and archiving/hard-delete never cascades records away. All gates green (432 tests, coverage/lint/typecheck/build).

userStories.title: Serve action on the counter screen
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to confirm the hand-out with one keystroke without leaving the counter screen. Source: tasks/prd-us-05-record-attendance.md §US-05.4
acceptanceCriteria: A German 'Ausgabe erfassen' button appears on /ausgabe only when the verdict permits serving
A 'paid' checkbox is pre-checked and can be cleared before confirming
The button is keyboard-reachable and confirms with Enter; on success a short confirmation shows and the lookup input re-focuses
A second attempt shows 'Heute bereits versorgt' with the existing record's time
A correct-today's-entry control allows changing the paid flag or removing the record, with a confirmation step before removal
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Extended lookupCustomer to read today's record in the same pass (added records port to its deps; new customerId + todaysRecord on CounterLookup; new domain helper attendance.recordForDay, with canRecord refactored onto it). Added src/app/ausgabe/actions.ts (recordServe/correctServe server actions), serve-state.ts, and the ServeControls client component: a pre-checked 'Bezahlt' checkbox + 'Ausgabe erfassen' button when the verdict permits serving; on success it revalidates, the branch switches to the already-served view (with the record's Berlin time via new germanTime), a green confirmation shows and the #counter-input clears+refocuses. Correction control (change paid / remove) with a native <details> confirmation step before removal. deps.ts now exposes read deps (no audit) + counterActionDeps (write, with audit). All strings in de.ts under distribution.serve. Gates green (lint/typecheck/build, 440 tests, 100% domain+application coverage). Runtime-verified by curl: CLEAR_TO_SERVE renders the serve button; after a recorded hand-out the same number renders 'Heute bereits versorgt um 13:10 Uhr (nicht bezahlt)' + correct/remove controls with the serve button gone. Full interactive flow (focus, checkbox, revalidate) is covered by US-005's Playwright spec — no dev-browser skill/MCP is configured here.

userStories.title: E2E: distribution-day happy path
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright spec covering the core distribution-day flow. Source: tasks/prd-us-05-record-attendance.md §US-05.5
acceptanceCriteria: Spec with a fixed clock on a Red distribution day looks up a Red customer, records attendance with paid, and asserts the confirmation and today's record
Spec attempts a second recording and asserts the German already-served message and that only one record exists
Spec clears the paid flag for a second customer and asserts the record stores paid = false
npm run test:e2e passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added tests/e2e/serve.spec.ts (serial, three cases) driving the distribution-day happy path through the built app against the real e2e SQLite file. Pinned to the RED Thursday 08.01.2026 via the FD_FIXED_NOW_FILE seam: looks up a RED household, presses 'Ausgabe erfassen' with the pre-checked Bezahlt box, asserts the German confirmation (Ausgabe um 10:00 Uhr — Berlin is UTC+1 in January) and the switch to today's record, then reads the row straight from Prisma to assert one record, paid=true, dayKey 2026-01-08. A second lookup finds the serve button gone and 'Heute bereits versorgt' in its place with still exactly one row (duplicate refused). A second household served with the box cleared stores paid=false. Households take numbers 221-222 to stay clear of counter.spec (201-206/239) and portions.spec (211) in the shared db. Gotcha: customerNumber is unique only via a hand-written partial index Prisma cannot see, so the DB read uses findFirst, not findUnique. All gates green: lint, typecheck, test:coverage (100% domain+application), build, and npm run test:e2e (24 passed, 1 pre-existing skip).
